title: What is MCP? Learn MCP Client, MCP Server & MCP Architecture
summary: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standard communication protocol that enables AI agents and applications to connect with external tools, APIs, databases, and services through a common interface. MCP defines client-server architecture using JSON-RPC messaging and supports local and remote server connections via STDIO or HTTP transport mechanisms.
writeup: This video explains the Model Context Protocol (MCP), a standard communication protocol that lets AI agents and applications connect to external tools, APIs, databases, and services through a common interface. It covers the client-server architecture, the use of JSON-RPC for messaging, and how MCP supports both local and remote server connections via STDIO or HTTP transport. The standardization matters because it replaces bespoke integrations with one consistent interface for linking models to outside systems.
